---
title: Median household income distributions differ by rurality
description: Density plot showing the distribution of median household income across
  rural and nonrural counties.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-median-hh-income-density/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Economic Indicators
  - Demographics
tags:
  - ACS
  - Median Household Income
  - Income Distribution
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Density Plot
image: X_median_hh_income_density.png
chart:
  title: Median household income distributions differ by rurality
  chartType: density
  interactive: false
  source: U.S. Census Bureau
  sourceUrl: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - american-community-survey
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This density plot compares the county-level distribution of median household income between rural and nonrural areas.](./X_median_hh_income_density.png)

## Overview

This density plot compares the county-level distribution of median household income between rural and nonrural areas.

## Key Findings

- Nonrural counties have higher median household incomes on average
- Rural income distribution is more concentrated at lower levels
- There is significant overlap between rural and nonrural distributions

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/med_hh_income_density.R` in the producing project.
